redirectFor.title: Redirect For
redirectFor.description: Specifies the type of user: authenticated or unauthenticated. Always redirects to the homepage.

isPrimaryContent.description: Determines if this block contains the H1 of the page, and should be prioritized in SEO and accessibility considerations
isPrimaryContent.title: Is Primary Content

tagAllowList.description: List of tags used to filter items for display in the component. Items must have at least one of the specified tags to be included.
tagAllowList.title: Allowed Tags

referenceFeed.description: Optional Single SubjectType feed ID. Provides the feed's dynamic path to construct page links when combined with items ids. If not provided, items will not link to pages automatically.
referenceFeed.title: Reference Feed

location.title: Carousel Location
location.description: Specifies where the carousel should be displayed on the page. This can be used to determine the layout and styling of the carousel based on its location.

items.name: Senior Developer
items.description:
- Collaborated on a new CMS frontend for the Rails API, migrating from a legacy Coldfusion CMS.
- Helped migrate much of the company's frontend components from Coldfusion/JQuery/SCSS to React/Nextjs/Tailwind in preparation for expansion into international markets.

items.name: Junior Backend Developer
items.description:
- Developed Rails JSON API endpoints supporting user account login.Some supported features: flexible requirements, content favoriting, magic links, daily emails.
- Modeled loyalty program tracking; used for event check-ins, coupon distribution, or interactive games. End users could log into the website through their phones and present a QR code to participate in onsite activities.
- Contributed to a GraphQL facade to serve content to Gatsby and the Nextjs websites.
- Used Rspec and Factorybot for testing.

items.name: Junior Frontend Developer
items.description:
- Collaborated with a team of graphic designers, translating Photoshop and Figma comps into reusable frontend components, shared across 500+ shopping center websites.
- Frontend components were composed of Coldfusion templates, with SCSS and JQuery providing artistic flourish and user experience.
- Responsibilities included:
    - Develop layouts with mixed content from retail brands, products, promotions, events, movies, press releases, job openings
    - 3rd party integrations(Google Ads, GTM, Facebook and other social networks, geojson maps, mailchimp, movie theater APIs, etc).
    - Account login and personalization experience.
    - Cross-browser compatibility, SEO optimization and ARIA compliance.

items.name: Metropolitan State University of Denver
